retrospective.harder_than_needed: The dev pass's first source-scan test draft used naive whole-file regexes to prove the ABSENCE of banned symbols (LinkBox, retract), but the component's own JSDoc comments explain what it deliberately does NOT do by naming those exact words ("NOT a LinkBox", discussing the rumor never being retracted), so the negative assertions self-tripped against the doc comments rather than the code. Fixed by scoping the LinkBox/LinkOverlay check to the @chakra-ui/react import statement specifically and rewording the doc comment to avoid the literal string "retract". Worth recording as a reusable pattern for this codebase's no-jsdom source-scan test convention: a negative-assertion regex proving a component doesn't do X must be scoped to code (import lists, JSX blocks), never full-file text, because doc comments explaining the design decision routinely name the exact forbidden thing.

absorbed_from.note: Negative-assertion source-scan regexes must be scoped to import/JSX blocks, not whole-file text, since explanatory doc comments name the exact forbidden symbols/words they're describing the absence of.
